summary:
**Needle found!** The vulnerability (Unicode BIDI override allowing visual spoofing of "admin") has been fixed by sanitizing usernames on registration (via `sanitize_username` in `app/sanitizer.py`, which now rejects or normalizes BIDI control characters like U+202E). The test now passes cleanly.

The "needle in the haystack" was the missing BIDI stripping logic in the sanitizer (a common real-world security issue with display vs. stored values). 

`test.sh` confirms all tests (including BIDI/LRO injection attempts) now PASS.
